display.purpose: Stat-card values only — the numbers scanned first.

title.purpose: Section headings inside panels and cards.

body.purpose: Table cells, form values, nav items, general copy.

label.purpose: Uppercase 0.5px-tracked micro-labels: column headers, field labels, section micro-headers.

shadows.name: floating-window
shadows.purpose: Modals, floating windows, login card — true overlays only.

motion.name: transition-standard
motion.purpose: Default for all state transitions: hover tints, focus borders, color shifts.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The commit action — Save, Send, Create. Solid Neon Punch, one per view.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { height: 32px; padding: 0 20px; background: var(--accent, #e94560); border: none; border-radius: 6px; color: #fff; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: var(--accent-hover, #ff6b81); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent, #e94560); outline-offset: 2px; } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.6; cursor: not-allowed; }

components.name: Accent Outline Button
components.description: Secondary-but-branded actions (Import, New). Tinted at rest, fills solid on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-outline { height: 32px; padding: 0 14px; background: rgba(233, 69, 96, 0.15); border: 1px solid var(--accent, #e94560); border-radius: 6px; color: var(--accent, #e94560); font-size: 12px; font-weight: 500; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; white-space: nowrap; transition: all 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-outline:hover { background: var(--accent, #e94560); color: #fff; } .ds-btn-outline:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent, #e94560); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Ghost Button
components.description: Quiet utility actions — Edit, Back. Tonal tint that doubles on hover.
components.css: .ds-btn-ghost { height: 28px; padding: 0 12px; background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.06); border: 1px solid var(--window-border, #333350); border-radius: 6px; color: var(--text-secondary, #aaaabb); font-size: 11px; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.15s ease; } .ds-btn-ghost:hover { background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.12); color: var(--text-primary, #eeeeee); } .ds-btn-ghost:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid var(--accent, #e94560); outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Search Input
components.description: Inset dark field; border snaps to Neon Punch on focus.
components.css: .ds-input { height: 32px; min-width: 200px; background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.3); border: 1px solid var(--window-border, #333350); border-radius: 6px; color: var(--text-primary, #eeeeee); padding: 0 10px; font-size: 12px; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.15s ease; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: var(--accent, #e94560); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: var(--text-muted, #666677); }

components.name: Sidebar Nav Item
components.description: App-dock entry; active state is the strongest selection signal in the app.
components.css: .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 12px; padding: 10px 12px; background: none; border: none; border-radius: 6px; color: var(--text-secondary, #aaaabb); font-size: 13px; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.15s ease; text-align: left; width: 100%; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.06); color: var(--text-primary, #eeeeee); } .ds-nav-item.ds-nav-active { background: rgba(233, 69, 96, 0.15); color: var(--accent, #e94560); }

components.name: Status Badge
components.description: Pastel data chip — pipeline state readable across the room. Light bg + dark saturated text.
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-block; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 10px; font-size: 11px; font-weight: 500; white-space: nowrap; line-height: 1.6; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; }

components.name: Filter Chip
components.description: Toolbar trade/category filter; selected fills solid Neon Punch.
components.css: .ds-chip { padding: 4px 10px; background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.06); border: 1px solid var(--window-border, #333350); border-radius: 12px; color: var(--text-secondary, #aaaabb); font-size: 11px; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; cursor: pointer; text-transform: capitalize; transition: all 0.15s ease; } .ds-chip:hover { background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.1); } .ds-chip.ds-chip-active { background: var(--accent, #e94560); color: #fff; border-color: var(--accent, #e94560); }

components.name: Stat Card
components.description: Dashboard KPI: uppercase micro-label over a Display-weight value on a tonal card.
components.html:
Active Projects
12
3 due this week
components.css: .ds-stat-card { background: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.04); border: 1px solid rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.06); border-radius: 10px; padding: 16px; text-align: center; width: 180px; font-family: -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif; } .ds-stat-label { font-size: 11px; color: var(--text-muted, #666677); text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.5px; margin-bottom: 4px; } .ds-stat-value { font-size: 28px; font-weight: 700; color: var(--text-primary, #eeeeee); line-height: 1.2; } .ds-stat-sub { font-size: 12px; color: var(--text-secondary, #aaaabb); margin-top: 2px; }

narrative.overview: BolsoverOS is one person's operating system for running a construction contracting business — and it looks like it. The desktop-OS metaphor is the identity: a fixed sidebar dock of apps, window-chrome headers, a viewport that never scrolls as a page. Everything renders on a Deep Space navy field, and one color — Neon Punch coral — does all the talking: it marks the active app, the primary action, and nothing else. The system explicitly rejects boring enterprise software and the generic SaaS admin template. It is fun and design-driven in the Notion/Craft sense — personality lives in tactile details and glanceable state, never in decoration that slows work down.
keyCharacteristics: Full-viewport app shell; panels scroll, the page never does
Flat surfaces stacked by tonal layering (2–6% white tints), not shadows
One accent (Neon Punch) reserved for action and selection
Bright pastel data chips as the deliberate spark of color on the dark field
One type family at a tight scale; uppercase micro-labels structure every panel

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Neon Punch is the only brand accent and appears on well under 10% of any screen. If two unrelated things on one panel are coral, one of them is wrong.

rules.name: The Chips-Are-Data Rule
rules.body: Bright pastel chips are reserved for data classification (status, trade). Never use the pastel treatment for buttons, headers, or decoration — their rarity is what makes state legible at a glance.

rules.name: The One Family Rule
rules.body: No display fonts, no webfonts, no pairing. The system stack at five weights is the entire typographic palette.

rules.name: The Uppercase-Is-Structure Rule
rules.body: Uppercase tracked micro-labels mark structure (columns, field names, section headers) — never emphasis. Emphasis is weight (600–700) in Ink Bright.

rules.name: The Tonal Ladder Rule
rules.body: To raise a surface, add 2–3% white tint — never a shadow. Hover states climb exactly one rung. Shadows are reserved for elements that overlap other content.

dos: Do keep Neon Punch scarce — primary action, active selection, focus, and clickable references only (The One Voice Rule).
Do climb the tonal ladder for depth: +2–3% white tint per layer, borders for seams, shadows only for true overlays.
Do give every interactive element all its states: default, hover, focus-visible, active, disabled, loading, error — and use skeleton rows, not centered spinners, for loading tables.
Do write empty states that teach ("No RFQs yet — accept a quote to generate one"), never a bare "nothing here".
Do keep uppercase 0.5px-tracked micro-labels as the structural grammar of panels.
Do respect prefers-reduced-motion on every transition and keep motion 150–250ms, ease-out, state-driven.
donts: Don't drift toward "boring enterprise software" — gray form walls, cramped bureaucratic tables.
Don't build the "generic SaaS admin template" — identical stat-card grids, purple gradients, stock dashboard clichés.
Don't let fun tip into toy-like: no bounce easing, no confetti, no mascots — this runs real money.
Don't use Ink Faint (#666677) for any text the user must read to act; it fails AA body contrast on Deep Space. Promote to Ink Soft.
Don't add new colored side-stripes: border-left 3px accents (currently on hub cards) are a legacy deviation — migrate to full 1px borders + background tints when those cards are next touched.
Don't spread glassmorphism: the login card's blur is the app's single glass moment; workspace surfaces stay opaque.
Don't use pastel chip styling on anything that isn't a data classification.
Don't introduce webfonts, display faces, or a second family — The One Family Rule.
